items.correct: The external macro-environment: Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Legal and Environmental factors affecting a business.
items.misconception: PESTLE analyses the internal strengths and weaknesses of a business.
items.random_wrong: PESTLE stands for Profit, Earnings, Sales, Trade, Loans, Equity.
items.why: Students confuse PESTLE (external) with SWOT (internal + external). PESTLE is about the wider environment a business cannot control; SWOT mixes internal and external.

items.correct: Output per unit of input (per worker, per hour) — measuring efficiency, not total output. A small efficient firm can be more productive than a large inefficient one.
items.misconception: Productivity is the total amount produced by a business.
items.random_wrong: Productivity is the number of employees a business has.
items.why: Total output and productivity are different. A factory of 100 workers producing 1000 units is less productive than 10 workers producing 200 units. The ratio matters, not the total.

items.q: What is a key disadvantage of being a sole trader?
items.correct: Unlimited liability — the owner's personal assets are at risk if the business fails, because there is no legal separation between owner and business.
items.misconception: The main disadvantage is that sole traders can't sell any products.
items.random_wrong: Sole traders must employ at least one other person by law.
items.why: Sole traders can sell products and don't need employees. The key legal disadvantage is unlimited liability — the personal risk exposure, which is why many businesses become limited companies as they grow.

items.q: Luca Pacioli's 'Summa de Arithmetica' detailing double-entry bookkeeping dates from:
items.why: It's 1494. “1390” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
The answer examiners want is: Net present value. “Payback” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Year Pacioli published 'Summa' codifying double-entry bookkeeping?
items.why: The real figure is 1494 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
